Other actors in the region have pledged to support Russia. Japan is considering how, and if, they should respond to the disaster. What should Japan do?
column2: Provide significant aid as well as any required emergency personnel to assist in responding to the earthquake in Russia
column3: Provide minimal aid to Russia to avoid the reputational consequences of doing nothing
column4: Do not get involved in committing national resources to another, well-resourced, state

column1: A public health crisis is emerging in United States. Medical experts have concluded that initial data demonstrates that the cause is a rapidly transferring viral infection. While experts remain uncertain about the seriousness of the virus, some have already died because of infection. Moreover, due to the ease in which the virus can be spread, some have concluded that a global crisis could quickly break out. However, there are politicians around the world who have played down the possible effects of the virus, suggesting that a global response is not required. Most medical experts disagree with this position and are calling for a rapid international effort to mitigate the possible public health crisis. How should Saudi Arabia respond?
column2: Do not commit resources to a possible crisis when the impact could turn out to be minimal
column3: Commit significant resources and technical experts to address the possible crisis as quickly as possible
column4: Commit minimal resources to support medical teams and research

Moreover, due to the ease in which the virus can be spread, some have concluded that a global crisis could quickly break out. However, there are politicians around the world who have played down the possible effects of the virus, suggesting that a global response is not required. Most medical experts disagree with this position and are calling for a rapid international effort to mitigate the possible public health crisis. How should Brazil respond?
